# Cure MCOPS12

> Austrian nonprofit funding RNA therapy research and drug repurposing for Syndromic Microphthalmia 12 (MCOPS12), a rare RARB-linked neurodevelopmental disorder.

Cure MCOPS12 was founded by the family of a child diagnosed with MCOPS12. Together with sister organizations A Cure for Sophia and Friends (US) and A Cure for Sienna (Australia), we fund research at IGBMC (France), CHU Sainte-Justine (Canada), AlphaRose RareLabs and NCATS (US). Our two scientific paths are (1) drug repurposing for symptomatic treatment and (2) an antisense oligonucleotide (ASO) therapy that silences the mutated RARB allele.

- [MCOPS12 Disease](/mcops12-disease): Symptoms and clinical course.
- [Plain Language MCOPS12](/plain-language-mcops12): Plain-language explanation.
- [Newly Diagnosed](/newly-diagnosed): Guide for new families.
- [Meet Our Kids](/meet-our-kids): Children living with MCOPS12.
- [Achievements](/achievements): Milestones since 2022.
- [Research](/research): Scientific strategy.
- [Funded R&D Programs](/funded-rd-programs): Funded research timeline.
- [Drug Repurposing](/drug-repurposing): Repurposing existing drugs.
- [ASO Therapy](/aso-therapy): Simon's antisense oligonucleotide program.
- [Natural History](/natural-history): The MCOPS12 Natural History Study.
- [Join Natural History Study](/join-natural-history-study): How patients can participate.

- [Scientific Advisory Board](/scientific-advisory-board): Our scientific advisors.
- [A Cure for Sophia and Friends](/a-cure-for-sophia-and-friends): US sister nonprofit.
- [A Cure for Sienna](/a-cure-for-sienna): Australian sister nonprofit.
- [News](/news): Latest updates.
- [How to Get Involved](/how-to-get-involved): Ways to help.
